1→"""Claude CLI prompt execution implementation. 2→ 3→This module provides the ClaudeCliPromptExecutor implementation and re-exports 4→ABC and types from erk_shared.core. 5→""" 6→ 7→from __future__ import annotations 8→ 9→import json 10→import logging 11→import os 12→import shutil 13→import subprocess 14→import sys 15→import threading 16→from collections.abc import Iterator 17→from pathlib import Path 18→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING 19→ 20→if TYPE_CHECKING: 21→ from erk_shared.context.types import PermissionMode 22→ 23→from erk_shared.context.types import permission_mode_to_claude 24→from erk_shared.core.prompt_executor import ( 25→ CommandResult, # noqa: F401 - re-exported for erk.cli.output 26→ ErrorEvent, 27→ ExecutorEvent, 28→ NoOutputEvent, 29→ NoTurnsEvent, 30→ PlanNumberEvent,
... [629 lines omitted] ...
32→ ProcessErrorEvent, 53→def format_prompt_error( 59→ """Format error message for failed prompt execution. 61→ Constructs a structured error message from subprocess failure details. 65→ stderr: Standard error output 69→ Formatted error string like "Exit code 1 | stderr: ... | stdout: ..." 71→ error_parts = [f"Exit code {returncode}"] 73→ error_parts.append(f"stderr: {stderr.strip()}") 76→ error_parts.append(f"stdout: {stdout_preview}") 77→ return " | ".join(error_parts) 153→ error_msg = f"Claude command {command} failed with exit code {result.returncode}" 154→ yield ErrorEvent(message=error_msg) 163→ # Handle Popen errors (e.g., claude not found, permission denied) 174→ except OSError as e: 175→ yield ProcessErrorEvent( 176→ message=f"Failed to start Claude CLI: {e}
Command: {' '.join(cmd_args)}" 282→ diag += "
 Run 'claude' directly to see hook error messages" 300→ yield ProcessErrorEvent( 318→ yield ErrorEvent(message=f"Exit code {returncode}") 321→ # Enhanced error messages for non-zero exit codes 323→ error_msg = f"Claude command {command} failed" 324→ error_msg += f"
 Exit code: {returncode}" 325→ error_msg += f"
 Lines processed: {line_count}" 327→ error_msg += "
 Stderr:
" + "".join(stderr_output).strip() 328→ yield ErrorEvent(message=error_msg) 364→ # Parse JSON safely - JSON parsing requires exception handling 371→ except json.JSONDecodeError: 386→ "is_error": None, 456→ result["is_error"] = data.get("is_error", False) 490→ raise RuntimeError("Claude CLI not found
Install from: https://claude.com/download") 495→ # FileNotFoundError which is the appropriate error. 527→ except OSError: 592→ error=f"Prompt execution timed out after {PROMPT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s", 599→ error=format_prompt_error( 609→ error=None,

1→"""Parser for Codex CLI JSONL streaming output. 2→ 3→Codex's --json output uses a two-level type system completely different from 4→Claude's stream-json format. This module provides a standalone parser that 5→converts Codex JSONL lines into erk's ExecutorEvent types. 6→ 7→See docs/learned/integrations/codex/codex-jsonl-format.md for format spec. 8→""" 9→ 10→from __future__ import annotations 11→ 12→import json 13→import logging 14→from pathlib import Path 15→ 16→from erk.core.output_filter import extract_pr_metadata_from_text 17→from erk_shared.core.prompt_executor import ( 18→ ErrorEvent, 19→ ExecutorEvent, 20→ PlanNumberEvent, 21→ PrNumberEvent, 22→ PrTitleEvent, 23→ PrUrlEvent, 24→ SpinnerUpdateEvent, 25→ TextEvent, 26→ ToolEvent, 27→) 28→ 29→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) 30→
... [301 lines omitted] ...
79→ if event_type == "turn.failed": 80→ return _handle_turn_failed(data) 81→ if event_type == "error": 82→ return _handle_top_level_error(data) 92→ """Parse JSON text, returning None on failure. 94→ JSON parsing is one of the few places where exception handling is 101→ except json.JSONDecodeError: 156→ """Handle item.completed events — emit TextEvent, ToolEvent, or ErrorEvent.""" 172→ if item_type == "error": 173→ message = item.get("message", "Unknown item error") 174→ return [ErrorEvent(message=str(message))] 279→ error = item.get("error") 283→ if error is not None and isinstance(error, str) and error.strip(): 284→ return [ToolEvent(summary=f"MCP {label}: error - {_truncate(error, 80)}")] 305→def _handle_turn_failed(data: dict) -> list[ExecutorEvent]: 306→ """Extract error from turn.failed event.""" 307→ error = data.get("error", {}) 308→ if isinstance(error, dict): 309→ message = error.get("message", "Turn failed (no message)") 311→ message = "Turn failed (no message)" 312→ return [ErrorEvent(message=str(message))] 315→def _handle_top_level_error(data: dict) -> list[ExecutorEvent]: 316→ """Extract error from top-level error event.""" 317→ message = data.get("message", "Unknown error") 318→ return [ErrorEvent(message=str(message))]

1→"""Output utilities for CLI commands with clear intent. 2→ 3→For user_output, machine_output, format_duration - import from erk_shared.output. 4→This module provides format_implement_summary and stream_command_with_feedback. 5→""" 6→ 7→from __future__ import annotations 8→ 9→import sys 10→import time 11→from dataclasses import dataclass 12→from pathlib import Path 13→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING 14→ 15→import click 16→from rich.panel import Panel 17→from rich.text import Text 18→ 19→if TYPE_CHECKING: 20→ from erk_shared.context.types import PermissionMode 21→ 22→from erk.core.prompt_executor import ( 23→ CommandResult, 24→ ErrorEvent, 25→ NoOutputEvent, 26→ NoTurnsEvent, 27→ PlanNumberEvent, 28→ PrNumberEvent, 29→ ProcessErrorEvent, 30→ PromptExecutor,
... [475 lines omitted] ...
41→ """Format final summary box with status, PR link, timing, errors. 65→ lines.append(Text("❌ Status: Failed", style="red")) 108→ # Error details (if failed) 112→ if result.error_message: 114→ lines.append(Text(f"Error in command {i + 1}:", style="red bold")) 115→ lines.append(Text(result.error_message, style="red")) 121→ title = "Implementation Complete" if overall_success else "Implementation Failed" 148→ - Error events: ` ! error message` (red) 150→ - End (failure): `--- Failed (1m 23s) ---` (red) 179→ error_message: str | None = None 224→ case ErrorEvent(message=msg): 226→ error_message = msg 230→ error_message = diag 234→ error_message = diag 236→ case ProcessErrorEvent(message=msg): 238→ error_message = msg 252→ click.echo(click.style(f"--- Failed ({duration_str}) ---", fg="red", bold=True), err=True) 261→ error_message=error_message, 271→ error_message: str | None = None 292→ RebaseResult with success status and error details 294→ error_message: str | None = None 342→ case ErrorEvent(message=msg): 344→ error_message = msg 348→ error_message = diag 352→ error_message = diag 354→ case ProcessErrorEvent(message=msg): 356→ error_message = msg 361→ # Check for no-work-events failure mode 364→ error_message = ( 368→ click.echo(click.style(f" {error_message}", fg="yellow")) 378→ click.echo(click.style(f"--- Failed ({duration_str}) ---", fg="red", bold=True)) 380→ return RebaseResult(success=success, error_message=error_message) 388→ error_message: str | None = None 411→ DivergenceFixResult with success status and error details 413→ error_message: str | None = None 461→ case ErrorEvent(message=msg): 463→ error_message = msg 467→ error_message = diag 471→ error_message = diag 473→ case ProcessErrorEvent(message=msg): 475→ error_message = msg 480→ # Check for no-work-events failure mode 483→ error_message = ( 487→ click.echo(click.style(f" {error_message}", fg="yellow")) 497→ click.echo(click.style(f"--- Failed ({duration_str}) ---", fg="red", bold=True)) 499→ return DivergenceFixResult(success=success, error_message=error_message)

1→"""Output filtering for Claude CLI stream-json format. 2→ 3→This module provides functions to parse and filter Claude CLI output in stream-json 4→format, extracting relevant text content and tool summaries while suppressing 5→verbose/noisy tool invocations. 6→""" 7→ 8→import json 9→from pathlib import Path 10→ 11→ 12→def extract_text_content(message: dict) -> str | None: 13→ """Extract Claude's text response from assistant message. 14→ 15→ Args: 16→ message: Assistant message dict from stream-json 17→ 18→ Returns: 19→ Extracted text content, or None if no text found 20→ 21→ Example: 22→ >>> msg = {"type": "assistant_message", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "Hello"}]} 23→ >>> extract_text_content(msg) 24→ 'Hello' 25→ """ 26→ content = message.get("content", []) 27→ if not isinstance(content, list): 28→ return None 29→ 30→ text_parts: list[str] = []

57→ # Test command failure 61→ ... except RuntimeError: 62→ ... print("Command failed as expected") 82→ simulated_process_error: str | None = None, 85→ simulated_prompt_error: str | None = None, 93→ command_should_fail: Whether execute_command should raise RuntimeError 102→ simulated_process_error: Error message to simulate process startup failure 109→ simulated_prompt_error: Error message to return from execute_prompt() on failure. 110→ If set, execute_prompt() will return a failure result. 113→ tests the "empty response" failure mode where Claude produces JSON 127→ self._simulated_process_error = simulated_process_error 133→ self._simulated_prompt_error = simulated_prompt_error 176→ RuntimeError: If command_should_fail was set to True 180→ # Process error takes precedence (simulates Popen failure) 181→ if self._simulated_process_error is not None: 182→ yield ProcessErrorEvent(message=self._simulated_process_error) 196→ diag += "\n Run 'claude' directly to see hook error messages" 202→ yield ErrorEvent(message=f"Claude command {command} failed (simulated failure)") 260→ RuntimeError: If command_should_fail was set to True 272→ error_message=f"Claude command {command} failed (simulated failure)", 283→ error_message=None, 304→ RuntimeError: If Claude CLI is not available 307→ raise RuntimeError("Claude CLI not found\nInstall from: https://claude.com/download") 361→ PromptResult with simulated output or error 365→ if self._simulated_prompt_error is not None: 369→ error=self._simulated_prompt_error, 386→ error=None,

1→"""Plan next steps formatting - single source of truth.""" 2→ 3→from dataclasses import dataclass 4→ 5→ 6→@dataclass(frozen=True) 7→class PlanNextSteps: 8→ """Canonical commands for plan operations.""" 9→ 10→ plan_number: int 11→ url: str 12→ 13→ @property 14→ def view(self) -> str: 15→ return self.url 16→ 17→ @property 18→ def dispatch(self) -> str: 19→ return f"erk pr dispatch {self.plan_number}" 20→ 21→ @property 22→ def checkout(self) -> str: 23→ return f"erk br co --for-plan {self.plan_number}" 24→ 25→ @property 26→ def checkout_new_slot(self) -> str: 27→ return f"erk br co --new-slot --for-plan {self.plan_number}" 28→ 29→ @property 30→ def dispatch_slash_command(self) -> str:
... [72 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Type definitions for GT kit operations.""" 2→ 3→from dataclasses import dataclass 4→from typing import Literal, NamedTuple 5→ 6→ 7→class CommandResult(NamedTuple): 8→ """Result from running a subprocess command. 9→ 10→ Attributes: 11→ success: True if command exited with code 0, False otherwise 12→ stdout: Standard output from the command 13→ stderr: Standard error from the command 14→ """ 15→ 16→ success: bool 17→ stdout: str 18→ stderr: str 19→ 20→ 21→# ============================================================================= 22→# Squash Operation Types 23→# ============================================================================= 24→ 25→ 26→@dataclass(frozen=True) 27→class SquashSuccess: 28→ """Success result from idempotent squash.""" 29→ 30→ success: Literal[True]
... [170 lines omitted] ...
37→class SquashError: 38→ """Error result from idempotent squash.""" 41→ error: Literal["no-commits", "squash-conflict", "squash-failed"] 56→LandPrErrorType = Literal[ 61→ "child-pr-reparent-failed", 62→ "github-api-error", 63→ "merge-failed", 78→class LandPrError: 79→ """Error result from landing a PR.""" 82→ error_type: LandPrErrorType 91→PrepErrorType = Literal[ 99→ "squash-failed", 118→class PrepError: 119→ """Error result from prep phase.""" 122→ error_type: PrepErrorType 131→PreAnalysisErrorType = Literal[ 137→ "squash-failed", 161→class PreAnalysisError: 162→ """Error result from pre-analysis phase.""" 165→ error_type: PreAnalysisErrorType 174→QuickSubmitErrorType = Literal["stage-failed", "commit-failed", "submit-failed"] 189→class QuickSubmitError: 190→ """Error result from quick-submit operation.""" 193→ error_type: QuickSubmitErrorType

1→"""Context types for erk and erk-kits. 2→ 3→This module provides the core data types used by ErkContext: 4→- RepoContext: Repository discovery result 5→- NoRepoSentinel: Sentinel for when not in a repository 6→- GlobalConfig: Global erk configuration 7→- LoadedConfig: Repository-level configuration 8→- InteractiveAgentConfig: Configuration for interactive agent launches (Claude or Codex) 9→- AgentBackend: Type for agent backend selection 10→- PermissionMode: Generic permission mode for both Claude and Codex 11→""" 12→ 13→from __future__ import annotations 14→ 15→from dataclasses import dataclass 16→from functools import cache 17→from pathlib import Path 18→from typing import Literal, Self 19→ 20→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId 21→ 22→# Agent backend types 23→AgentBackend = Literal["claude", "codex"] 24→ 25→# Claude CLI permission modes: 26→# - "default": Default mode with permission prompts 27→# - "acceptEdits": Accept edits without prompts (--permission-mode acceptEdits) 28→# - "plan": Plan mode for exploration and planning (--permission-mode plan) 29→# - "bypassPermissions": Bypass all permissions (--permission-mode bypassPermissions) 30→ClaudePermissionMode = Literal["default", "acceptEdits", "plan", "bypassPermissions"]
... [303 lines omitted] ...
51→ raise ValueError(f"Unknown permission_mode: {permission_mode}") 75→ raise ValueError(f"Unknown permission_mode: {permission_mode}") 99→ raise ValueError(f"Unknown permission_mode: {permission_mode}")
